page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is an x86_64 architecture instance belonging to the ecs.m1 server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, operating with a single thread per core. The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage volumes. It runs on the KVM hypervisor and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.m1.medium is designed for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for application servers, web serving, mid-range databases, and development environments that require dedicated CPU resources without specialized acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring consistent compute performance. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. Lacking advanced vector extensions or cryptographic hardware acceleration flags in its configuration, this instance is optimized for standard workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including web servers, code repositories, and lightweight database deployments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and KVM virtualization, designed for standard workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and KVM virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual instance offering a balanced resource profile for standard application hosting and general-purpose workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
